overview: The Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee approved two rezoning petitions and voted to forward a modified fee waiver resolution to the county board that would only waive reconnect fees for septic systems damaged by the April 17, 2026 tornado. The committee also received educational presentations from Solid Waste, Parks Recreation and Forestry, and Conservation Planning and Zoning departments.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Public hearing - Tim Veland rezoning petition (urban residential/general agriculture to rural residential, Town of Green Valley)
Public hearing - Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition (Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture, Town of McMillan)
Fee waiver consideration for April 17, 2026 severe weather impacted properties
Department overview - Parks, Recreation and Forestry (Tom Lovelin)
Department overview - Conservation Planning and Zoning (Lori Miskimmons)

discussions.body: Supervisor Sefeld made a motion to approve the minutes, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck. The motion carried with no opposition on a voice vote.

discussions.item: Tim Veland rezoning petition - Town of Green Valley
discussions.body: Land Use Specialist Garrett Pogle presented the staff report for rezoning from urban residential and general agriculture to rural residential. The Town of Green Valley recommended approval on March 10, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Drebeck moved to approve based on findings of fact and conclusions of law, seconded by Supervisor Kurt. Motion carried unanimously.

discussions.item: Jeffrey and Cynthia Schmidtz rezoning petition - Town of McMillan
discussions.body: Garrett Pogle presented the petition to rezone from Conservancy Recreation to General Agriculture. Supervisor Leur asked about the conservancy nature and whether natural areas would be lost. Pogle explained the property contains a home and horse riding stable, and rezoning would make the home a conforming use rather than non-conforming. The Town of McMillan recommended approval on April 20, 2026. No public testimony was offered. Supervisor Sefeld moved to approve, seconded by Supervisor Drew. Motion carried unanimously.

Director Lori Miskimmons presented a proposal to waive county zoning and septic fees for properties damaged by the April 17, 2026 tornado. Supervisor Drew questioned where to draw the line for future events. Supervisor Leur asked about tracking costs. Supervisor Drebeck expressed concern about fairness to individual tornado victims not in declared disaster areas. After discussion, Supervisor Sefeld moved to forward the resolution. Supervisor Drebeck then amended the motion to only waive septic system reconnect fees ($160), not other zoning or septic fees. The amendment was seconded by Supervisor Kurt and passed. The amended resolution then passed on a non-unanimous voice vote to forward to the county board.

Dave Hagenbusher presented Solid Waste overview covering landfill operations, renewable natural gas partnership with Vidi Energy, leachate treatment requirements by 2032, and capital projects. Tom Lovelin presented Parks, Recreation and Forestry overview noting 44 full-time and 120 seasonal staff, 17 county parks, and 30,700 acres of county forest. Lovelin announced his retirement effective June 1st after nearly 30 years. Lori Miskimmons began the Conservation Planning and Zoning presentation before the transcript ended.

actionItems: Forward amended fee waiver resolution to county board - waiving only septic reconnect fees for tornado-impacted properties
Rezoning for Offer/Tract property forwarded to county board for approval
Rezoning for Schmidtz property forwarded to county board for approval
Staff to track costs associated with tornado-related permit requests
Tom Lovelin retiring June 1, 2026 - Joe Tucker selected as new county forest administrator
title: Marathon County Environmental Resources Committee Meeting
